title: Oil prices cool after mutual halt: WTI +0.86% to $91.32, Brent +1.65% to $94.63 (earlier spike to $96.24); Hormuz June reopening 'unlikely', Fitch: end of July
snippet: Oil prices retreated from earlier highs as the mutual halt eased tensions. WTI settled +0.86% at $91.32, Brent +1.65% at $94.63, after earlier intraday spikes to $93.41 and $96.24 respectively. Analysts say Hormuz June reopening is 'unlikely'; Fitch estimates end of July at earliest. The blockade continues to support elevated price floor. Covered by TradingKey, IndexBox, CNBC, Reuters.
tag_reason: Continuation of oil price tracking from src-1638 (Brent +3.6% to $96.47 on June 8); prices retreated from spike as mutual halt eased immediate tensions; Hormuz reopening timeline is new information
